title: Oral support for micro scanning and analysis of sublingual microcirculation
abstract: This invention provides stability and pressure control for sublingual blood microcirculation imaging devices, which support comprises a transparent tip (1) and an oral support (2), joined by an outer thread (3) of the tip (1) and inner thread (11) of an inclined seat (11); once the tip (1) is inserted into the support (2), the outer thread (3) rotates around the inner thread (12) of the seat (11) while the tip (1) passes through the oblong hole (13) to reach the sublingual region where blood microcirculation is being studied, and the adjustment of the tip depth (1); the optical tip (P) of the microscope (M) takes place through the thread (3,12) without exacerbated pressure; includes a constructive alternative with inner (40) and outer (33) rings configuring an orbital adjustment mechanism, which allows also angular adjustment of the tip (21).
claims:
Claims CLAIM 1) âORAL SUPPORT FOR MICRO SCANNING AND ANALYSIS OF SUBLINGUAL MICROCIRCULATIONâ characterized bv consisting of a transparent tip (1 ) that can be coupled and coated with an optical tip (P) of a sublingual imaging microscope (M), and at the same time can be coupled to an oral support (2), by means of an outer thread (3), where this support (2) has a horizontal extension (4) consisting of a top surface (5) and a bottom surface (6), where the top surface (5) has through-holes (7) that can receive a fast-drying dental modeling clay (8), and the bottom surface (6) has holes (9) that can also receive the dental modeling clay (8); the oral support (2) has a convex flap (10) that acts as a contact bulkhead with the patient's lips, when the horizontal extension (4) is inserted into the patient's mouth; from this convex flap (10), an inclined seat is projected (11), provided with an inner thread (12) that acts as a base to receive the outer thread (3) of the disposable transparent tip (1 ) and, likewise, the bottom surface (6) of the horizontal extension (4) has an elongated hole (13); once the tip (1 ) has been inserted into this support (2), the outer thread (3) rotates around the inner thread (12) of the seat (11 ) of the oral support (2) at the same time that this tip (1 ) passes through the oblong hole (13) until it reaches the sublingual region of the patient where the blood microcirculation is to be studied, and adjustment of the depth of the tip (1 ) and consequently of the optical tip (P) of the microscope (M) takes place through the thread (3, 12) without any pressure on the area to be studied. 2) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 1, characterized bv the fact that the tip (1 ) has vertical grooves (14) that act as knurling and contact point for the tip (1 ) to rotate around the thread (12) of the oral support (2). 

3) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the tip (1 ) is disposable. 4) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the convex flap (10) of the oral support (2) is provided with "T" couplings (15), which can receive a fastening and support strap (16) that supports and fastens the entire oral support (2) assembly on the patient's head. 5) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 1, characterized bv the fact that before inserting the oral support (2) into the patient's mouth, the oral support (2) receives the fast-drying dental modeling clay (8), deposited on the top surface (6) and consequently on the holes (7), as well as on the horizontal opening (9) of the bottom surface (6) and, by inserting the oral support (2), the patient's dental arch presses the modeling clay (8) and, after fast drying, configures a fitting template of this oral support (2) for further use on the same patient, if necessary, without losing the microscope reading position (M) through the optical tip (P). 6) âORAL SUPPORT FOR MICRO SCANNING AND ANALYSIS OF SUBLINGUAL MICROCIRCULATIONâ according to claim 1 and in a first constructive alternative characterized by comprising a transparent tip (21 ) that can be coupled and coated with an optical tip (P) of a sublingual imaging microscope (M), being at the same time coupled to an oral support (22), by means of an outer thread (23); the support (22) has a horizontal extension (24) consisting of a top surface (25) and a bottom surface (26), where the top surface (25) has through-holes (27) that can receive a fast-drying dental modeling clay (28), and the bottom surface (26) has holes (29) that can also receive the dental modeling clay (28); this oral support (22) has a convex flap (30) that acts as a contact bulkhead with the patient's lips, when the horizontal extension (24) is inserted 

into the patient's mouth; from the end of the horizontal extension (24), bearings (31 ) are seated with a threaded adjustment screw (32), and from these bearings (31 ), an outer ring (33) is attached that can be pivoted. The outer ring (33) has a horizontal shaft (34) which is housed inside the bearing (31 ) and, at the opposite end, the screw shaft (32) rotates around a threaded hole (35), which provides tightening after selection of the desired position; the lower end (36) of said outer ring (33) has a threaded hole (37) and houses a threaded shaft (38) driven by screw (39) from which it is housed and is capable of pivoting a circular inner ring (40), orthogonal to the bearings (31 ) of the outer ring (33), thus forming a mechanism of orbital movement; the outer ring (40) has a vertical shaft (41) that is housed inside a hole (42) of the outer ring (33) and around which it can pivot the inner ring (40), and the screw drive (39) promotes the tightening and locking of the inner ring (40) in the outer ring (33) from the threaded shaft (38), which provides the tightening after selecting the desired position; the internal ring (40) has an inner thread (42) that acts as seat to receive the outer thread (23) of the disposable transparent tip (21) and, in the same way, the bottom surface (26) of the horizontal extension (24) has an opening (43); once the tip (21 ) is inserted into the support (22), the outer thread (23) rotates around the inner thread (42) of the inner ring (40) at the same time that the tip (21 ) crosses the opening (43) until it reaches the sublingual region of the patient where the blood microcirculation is being studied, without pressing on the area under study. 7) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 6, characterized by the fact that the depth of the tip (21 ) and consequently of the optical tip (P) of the microscope (M) is regulated through the thread (23, 42). The tip (21 ) has vertical grooves (44) that act as knurling and contact point for the tip to rotate (21) around the thread 

(42) of the inner ring (40). 8) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 6, characterized by the fact that the angular adjustment of the tip (21 ) is done through the orbital movement of the outer rings (33) and inner rings (40) until the selection of the desired position for the reading and consequent drive of the fixing screws (32,39) respectively. 9) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 6, characterized bv the fact that the tip (21 ) is disposable. 10) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 6. characterized bv the fact that the convex flap (30) is provided with "T" couplings (45), which can receive a fastening and support strap (46) that supports and fastens the entire oral support (22) assembly on the patient's head. 11) âORAL SUPPORTâ, according to claim 6, characterized bv the fact that before inserting the oral support (22) into the patient's mouth, the oral support (22) receives the fast-drying dental modeling clay (28), deposited on the top surface (25) and consequently on the holes (27), as well as on the holes (29) of the bottom surface (26) and, by inserting the oral support (22), the patient's dental arch presses the modeling clay (28) and, after fast drying, configures a fitting template of this oral support (22) for further use on the same patient, if necessary, without losing the microscope reading position (M) through the optical tip (P).
